title: C User Input
content:
### User Input in C

The `scanf()` function from `<stdio.h>` reads user input from the keyboard.

### Using scanf()

```c
scanf("format specifier", &variable);
```

The `&` (address-of) operator gives the memory address of the variable.

### Format Specifiers for Input

| Specifier | Variable Type |
|-----------|--------------|
| `%d` | `int` |
| `%f` | `float` |
| `%lf` | `double` |
| `%c` | `char` |
| `%s` | char array (string) |

### Input Tips

| Situation | Solution |
|-----------|----------|
| String with spaces | Use `fgets()` instead of `scanf()` |
| Clearing input buffer | Use `while (getchar() != '\n');` |
| Reading a line | `fgets(buffer, size, stdin)` |
| Reading a character | `getchar()` function |

---

> **Key Point:** Always use `&` before variables in `scanf()`. For strings (char arrays), the array name acts as a pointer so `&` is not needed.

quiz.question: What operator is needed before a variable in scanf()?
